transcript: higher minimum wage pay to more required sick days. KTVU's Ali Rasmus back in studio to detail some of the laws that are likely going to have a big impact, Ali. Yeah, and a lot of those new laws affect conditions in the workplace, starting with more required sick pay. Starting in 2024, employers will be required to provide at least five days of paid sick leave to their workers. That's an increase of from just three days last year. Another law forbids employers from firing workers who use cannabis in their off-time hours. And another work-related law that goes into effect next year forbids workers from being penalized or retaliated against by their employers for asking their co-workers about their pay or encouraging others they work with to ask for a raise. And speaking of wages, California's minimum wage will increase to $16 an hour for all employees January 1st. That's 50 cents more than the required minimum last year. Other laws passed require fast food workers to be paid a minimum wage of $20 an hour. And the state's lowest paid health care workers, including janitors and medical techs, will have a new minimum wage of $25 an hour starting in 2024, although that pay increase will be phased in over a two-year period. Other new laws that go into effect eliminate so-called added fees for concerts and other events. There's also laws about the taxes on firearms and a law that will make it easier for renters to pay their security deposits to move into a new place. We'll detail those new laws coming up in the next hour of Mornings on 2.
